title: Distinction
content:
"I am but clay," the sinner plead,
    Who fed each vain desire.
    "Not only clay," another said,
    "But worse, for thou art mire."
meaning:
This evocative piece by Paul Laurence Dunbar, titled "Distinction", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
